sources: CALCULATION NOTE: In Historical mode, stored returns are TOTAL RETURNS net of MER (sourced from Yahoo Finance adjusted close prices or fund factsheets which report after-fee returns). MER is NOT deducted again in the calculation to avoid double-counting. In Projection mode, the annualReturn field is a gross estimate and MER IS deducted.

IMPORTANT DATA NOTE (March 2026): Annual returns for VAS, A200, IOZ, STW have been corrected to total returns (price appreciation + dividends reinvested), sourced from Yahoo Finance adjusted close prices. Previous values were price-only (excluding ~4.5% p.a. dividends) which significantly understated returns. Run .github/workflows/scripts/update_etf_data.py via GitHub Actions for automatic updates.
Annual total returns sourced from ASX fund factsheets and issuer websites (Vanguard AU, BetaShares, iShares, VanEck).
MER figures from current product disclosure statements (PDS) on each issuer's website.
Dividend yields are trailing 12-month estimates from ASX announcements and fund distributions.
Sector weightings from fund holdings disclosures (end-of-prior-quarter).
Top holdings reflect approximate current weights from issuer monthly disclosures.

1970-2024 asset class returns: AU Shares approximates ASX 200/All Ordinaries total return; Intl Shares approximates MSCI World ex-AU in AUD; AU Bonds approximates Bloomberg AusBond Composite; Cash approximates RBA overnight cash rate.

1901-1969 asset class returns are HIGHLY APPROXIMATE estimates derived from academic historical studies (Dimson/Marsh/Staunton 'Triumph of the Optimists', various RBA historical papers). These figures carry substantially higher uncertainty and should be treated as rough order-of-magnitude guides only.

Data before 1970 for Australian markets is particularly unreliable — reliable continuous price series for Australian equities do not exist before the 1970s. Pre-1970 figures are reconstructed estimates and may differ significantly from reality.
Reliable Australian share market data does NOT exist back to the 1800s. The earliest reasonably continuous Australian equity data begins in the 1880s-1900s at best.
Composite returns for diversified funds (VDHG, DHHF etc.) are estimated by blending asset class histories per published strategic asset allocations.
All returns in AUD. Past performance does not guarantee future results. Always read the PDS.

100US.label: 100% US Shares (S&P 500 / CRSP)
100US.description: 100% US equity. Tracks S&P 500 / CRSP US Total Market (VTS, IVV). USD-unhedged — includes AUD/USD currency fluctuations. Higher historical growth than AU or global broad market, but concentrated single-country risk.

AU50_US50.label: 50% AU / 50% US Shares
AU50_US50.description: A common simple two-fund portfolio combining Australian equity (VAS/A200) and US equity (VTS/IVV). Captures AU franking credits + high US growth. No bonds — higher volatility.

VGS.name: Vanguard MSCI World ex-AU
VGS.returnsNote: historicalReturns are total returns (price + dividends reinvested) derived from Vanguard VGS.AX adjusted-close prices via Yahoo Finance, matching Streamlit ETF calculator reference values. annualReturn used for projection mode is a conservative long-term estimate; the 2015-2026 CAGR was 16.7% (unusually strong period for global equities).

VDHG.name: Vanguard Diversified High Growth
VDHG.holdingsNote: Constituent stocks estimated by mapping VDHG's underlying fund allocations (36% VAS, 26.5% VGS-equiv, 5.5% VGE, 5.5% VISM, bonds/cash 26.5%) through to individual stock weights.
VDHG.returnsSource: Vanguard Australia published fund returns (after fees, AUD, calendar year). Updated by GitHub Action with Yahoo Finance adjusted-close data.

DHHF.name: BetaShares Diversified All Growth
DHHF.holdingsNote: Constituent stocks estimated from 37% AU Shares, 40% Intl Shares, 23% AU Equity via IAF/BNKS/FOOD.
DHHF.returnsSource: BetaShares published fund returns (after fees, AUD). Updated by GitHub Action.

DJRE.name: SPDR Dow Jones Global Real Estate

QPON.name: BetaShares Australian Bank Senior Floating Rate Bond

AFI.name: Aust. Foundation Investment Co.
AFI.description: Oldest and largest ASX LIC. Actively managed diversified Australian equities, concentrated large-cap portfolio. Pays fully franked dividends. Compare vs VAS/IOZ to see active vs passive performance.

ARG.name: Argo Investments
ARG.description: Second largest ASX LIC. Actively managed diversified Australian equities, similar holdings to AFI and passive AU index. Fully franked dividends.

WAM.name: WAM Capital
WAM.description: Active Australian small & mid-cap LIC managed by Geoff Wilson. Higher MER than passive funds. Performance has been mixed in recent years. Pays fully franked dividends from income and capital gains.

WGB.name: WAM Leaders
WGB.description: Active Australian large-cap LIC by Wilson Asset Management. Lower MER than WAM Capital, targeting ASX 200 large-caps. Compare vs VAS/IOZ for active-vs-passive insight.

MFF.name: MFF Capital Investments
MFF.description: Actively managed global equity LIC by Chris Mackay (formerly Magellan). Concentrated portfolio of international quality businesses. Strong long-run track record vs VGS/QUAL; higher MER.

PMC.name: Platinum Capital
PMC.description: Active global equity LIC with a contrarian, value-oriented approach. Has significantly underperformed passive international ETFs over the last decade. Useful for illustrating the cost of active management with underperformance.

VTS.name: US Total Market ETF
VTS.description: Tracks the CRSP US Total Market Index — the broadest US equity index (~3,700 stocks). Currency unhedged (USD exposure). Oldest Vanguard ETF on ASX (est. 2009). Note: treated as a US trust for tax; distributions may have different treatment — see your tax adviser.
